figures.caption: Table 8: 2D Darcy Flow Ablation–basis size M

Figure 1: From global to adaptive spectral representations. FNO operates on fixed Fourier modes eik⋅x in k-space, while ABLE lifts the representation to (k, y) via learned basis functions ek,y(x) = √ p(x, y)eik⋅x, enabling localized, data-dependent operator learning.

Table 1: Results on 1D Burgers equation (s = 256). ABLE attains the best accuracy across viscosity regimes, with the largest gains in the challenging low-viscosity case (ν = 10−3). Best results (lowest error) are highlighted in green.

Table 2: Results on 2D Darcy flow (s = 141 × 141). ABLE improves standard spectral operators, and further enhances hybrid architectures (U-FNO→ U-ABLE), achieving the best overall accuracy. Best results (lowest error) are highlighted in green.
Table 5: Results on 1D Burgers equation s = 256
Table 3: Results on 2D Navier–Stokes (ν = 10−5). ABLE improves spectral baselines and further enhances hybrid architectures, achieving the best accuracy.
